sections.paragraph:
m.-

(1) an apple (so called from its scent; root נָפַח compare Son 7:9), Pro 25:11 also an apple tree, Son 2:3 8:5 . (Arab. تُفَّاحُ an apple, not only a common one, but also the lemon, citron, etc.)

(2) [Tappuah], pr.n. (“a place fruitful in apples”).-(a) of a town in the tribe of Judah, Jos 12:17 15:34 .-(b) on the confines of Ephraim and Manasseh, Jos 16:8.-(c) m. 1Ch 2:43.
